posts.thread_title: Surfer’s Ear: ear health tips?  - safest way for me to clean my ears?
posts.author: riksa
posts.content: There has to be a lot of marketing bs out there as far as devices go. I think there is overkill and overpricing, even Jack says you can just get a TENS unit. A TENS unit with some settings and earclip electrodes 70€, or used and DIY like me for 30€ total. Or you can buy nurosym for 700€ which is basically the same thing but with an integrated app and marketing for tVNS. If money is no issue there is no reason to overthink it, just buy the tVNS device you like, they'll likely all be customizable adequately.

posts.thread_title: Albert's Optimal Journal with wife and toddlers
posts.author: AlbertW
posts.content: Toddler eczema still isn't improving much. Recently I read Jack's blog about building solar callus. - IR-A, morning sun: we've been getting toddler into the sun around sunrise - feeding him a lot of king salmon collar recently: he likes it and it's super high in DHA - going to try out seafood exoskeleton broth We just started supplementing with L-histidine yesterday, hoping to help with the filaggrin. There are multiple studies showing positive impact and no side effects. Of course getting it via AM sun exposure is best, but we're hoping that this will help him along.

posts.thread_title: I Stay Away...From nnEMF
posts.author: Anthony Smith
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.
